text.title: Global Exception Handler and Antipatterns
text.summary: @RestControllerAdvice with ProblemDetail (RFC 7807), validation error mapping, and Jakarta namespace gotchas.

rule.statement: Handle all exceptions in a @RestControllerAdvice returning ProblemDetail (RFC 7807). Never return custom JSON shapes for errors — use the standard type/title/status/detail structure.
rule.rationale: ProblemDetail is Spring Boot 3's built-in RFC 7807 implementation. Using custom error shapes forces clients to maintain two parsers. ProblemDetail is standardised and extensible via setProperty.

rule.statement: Spring Boot 3 uses jakarta.* exclusively. Never mix javax.* imports. jakarta.persistence.*, jakarta.validation.*, jakarta.servlet.* are the correct packages.
rule.rationale: LLMs trained on Spring Boot 2.x examples emit javax.* imports. These fail to compile with Spring Boot 3 dependencies. Pin the namespace in the prompt to prevent the mix.

rule.statement: Always use constructor injection (@RequiredArgsConstructor on final fields). Never use field injection (@Autowired on non-final field). Constructor injection makes dependencies visible and testable.
rule.rationale: Field injection hides dependencies and makes unit testing without a Spring context impossible. @RequiredArgsConstructor + final fields is the idiomatic Spring Boot 3 pattern.

text.reference: Test helper detecting N+1 via Hibernate Statistics
